commands.repair_reason: This needs contributor or maintainer refresh plus real behavior proof before automation can safely help; the missing proof gate and local-file security boundary make it human-handled for now.; Needs attention: the diff touches a local-file-serving boundary and currently diverges from shared avatar containment and size-limit policy.; Review finding: [P1] Keep current avatar resolver contracts — src/agents/identity-av... (sha=5da7f39eef798b4be8e80917f45e294a3e33aec6)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds IDENTITY.md avatar backtick parsing, relative local avatar path normalization, resolver tests, a changelog entry, and unrelated onPayload type casts.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level for the backtick-wrapped IDENTITY.md avatar case: current main preserves the backticks, which can make local resolution fail. The original Windows no-backtick report was not live-reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: This needs contributor or maintainer refresh plus real behavior proof before automation can safely help; the missing proof gate and local-file security boundary make it human-handled for now.

target.title: Fix/38439 webchat avatar identity md
target.body:
## Summary
  - **Problem:** `GET /avatar/{agentId}` returns 404 when the avatar is set only in the agent 
  workspace `IDENTITY.md` (no `agents.list[].identity.avatar`). Relative avatar paths were not
   normalized; backtick-wrapped values in IDENTITY.md were not parsed.
  - **Why it matters:** Control UI / webchat shows broken avatars for agents configured via 
  IDENTITY.md only (e.g. custom workspace, `Avatar: avatars/gaia.png`).
  - **What changed:** Normalize relative path in `resolveLocalAvatarPath` for cross-platform 
  resolution; strip surrounding backticks when parsing IDENTITY.md avatar value; add tests for
   backtick parsing and for non-default agent resolving avatar from IDENTITY.md only.
  - **What did NOT change:** No config/API changes; gateway still uses `loadConfig()` and 
  `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir` as before.
  ## Change Type (select all)
  - [x] Bug fix
  - [ ] Feature
  - [ ] Refactor
  - [ ] Docs
  - [ ] Security hardening
  - [ ] Chore/infra
  ## Scope (select all touched areas)
  - [x] Gateway / orchestration
  - [ ] Skills / tool execution
  - [ ] Auth / tokens
  - [ ] Memory / storage
  - [ ] Integrations
  - [ ] API / contracts
  - [x] UI / DX
  - [ ] CI/CD / infra
  ## Linked Issue/PR
  - Closes #38439
  - Related #
  ## User-visible / Behavior Changes
  `/avatar/{agentId}` and `/avatar/{agentId}?meta=1` now return 200 (and serve the image or 
  meta JSON) when the avatar is defined only in the agent workspace `IDENTITY.md` and the file
   exists. No config or CLI changes.
  ## Security Impact (required)
  - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
  - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
  - New/changed network calls? (`No`)
  - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
  - Data access scope changed? (`No`)
  - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A
  ## Repro + Verification
  ### Environment
  - OS: Windows 11 (from issue); also verified on Unix (tests).
  - Runtime/container: Node 22, local gateway.
  - Integration/channel: Control UI / webchat bound to agent with custom workspace.
  ### Steps
  1. Add agent (e.g. `gaia`) with `workspace` set; do not set `identity.avatar` in config.
  2. In that workspace, create `IDENTITY.md` with `- **Avatar:** avatars/gaia.png` and place 
  `avatars/gaia.png`.
  3. Restart gateway; open Control UI; send a message.
  ### Expected
  Avatar image loads; `GET /avatar/gaia` returns 200 with image body.
  ### Actual (before fix)
  404 for `GET /avatar/gaia` and `GET /avatar/gaia?meta=1`.
  ## Evidence
  - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after: New unit tests for IDENTITY.md backtick 
  parsing and for non-default agent (gaia) resolving avatar from IDENTITY.md only; existing
  `identity-avatar.e2e.test.ts` and `identity-file.e2e.test.ts` cover the change.
  ## Human Verification (required)
  - Verified scenarios: Ran `pnpm check`; ran `pnpm test -- 
  src/agents/identity-avatar.e2e.test.ts src/agents/identity-file.e2e.test.ts --run`.
  - Edge cases checked: Relative path normalization (no absolute/`~` change); backtick 
  stripping only when value is wrapped in single backticks.
  - What you did **not** verify: Live gateway on Windows with real config (issue reporter’s 
  exact env).
  ## Compatibility / Migration
  - Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
  - Config/env changes? (`No`)
  - Migration needed? (`No`)
  - If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A
  ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)
  - Revert the PR or the single commit; no config to restore.
  - Known bad symptoms: If 404 returns for avatars that previously worked, check that 
  workspace path and IDENTITY.md path are unchanged and that `resolveAgentWorkspaceDir` still 
  points at the same directory.
  ## Risks and Mitigations
  None. Change is limited to avatar resolution and IDENTITY.md parsing; no new network or file
   access outside existing workspace.
target.author: alphadl

target.title: feat(plugins): add optional api.resetSession()
target.body:
## Summary

`main` already has gateway-side session reset support through `performGatewaySessionReset()`.

This PR adds optional plugin-side `api.resetSession(key, reason?)` support on top of the current mainline architecture.

- Adds `PluginResetSessionResult` type and optional `resetSession` on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Wires `api.resetSession(...)` in the plugin registry only in full registration mode and only when the gateway exposes `sessions.reset`
- Reuses gateway-owned canonicalization and reset resolution (no plugin-side duplicate logic)
- Unifies config snapshot usage in `performGatewaySessionReset` to eliminate config-drift between target resolution and entry lookup
- Adds per-canonical-key in-flight guard in the reset service
- Exports `PluginResetSessionResult` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`

## Scope

This PR:
- adds `PluginResetSessionResult` to `src/plugins/types.ts`
- adds optional `resetSession` to `OpenClawPluginApi`
- adds builder support in `src/plugins/api-builder.ts`
- wires `api.resetSession(...)` in `src/plugins/registry.ts` (full-mode + capability gated)
- adds optional `existingCfg` param to `loadSessionEntry` in `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- adds in-flight guard and unified resolution in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`
- exports the result type from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
- adds focused plugin contract tests (17 tests)
- refreshes generated plugin SDK API baselines

This PR does not:
- change gateway reset RPC behavior
- broaden session architecture scope beyond the plugin API surface
- revive any stale-branch refactor baggage from the closed #43375

## Behavior

`api.resetSession(key, reason?)`:
- accepts `"new"` or `"reset"` reason values (defaults to `"new"`)
- trims and validates the key
- delegates to gateway-owned `performGatewaySessionReset`
- resolves to a structured plugin result instead of rejecting for operational failures
- absent in setup-only, setup-runtime, and cli-metadata registration modes
- absent when gateway does not expose `sessions.reset`

Success: `{ ok: true, key, sessionId }`
Failure: `{ ok: false, key, code, message }`

## Local Verification

```
pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts     # 17/17 pass
pnpm test -- src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts      # 77/77 pass
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check                           # OK
pnpm tsgo                                           # no new errors
```

Replaces #43375 (closed, stale branch).

commands.freeform_prompt: quick follow-up: the latest pushed head is now 6f4d20af34 after fixing the CI type annotations from the rerun. All feedback remains addressed: - Reset broadcasts are preserved for `sessions.reset` and the reset path now keeps a consistent config snapshot when reloading the entry under the store lock. - `api.resetSession(key, reason?)` is documented in the plugin runtime docs. - The changelog includes the new plugin SDK reset-session API entry. - The plugin SDK API baseline was regenerated and checked. Focused local validation on the final head: - `pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts` - `pnpm test -- src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-hooks.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - Direct `tsgo` core + extensions checks using the installed binaries, because this worktree has broken `.bin` symlink resolution for `pnpm exec` after reinstall. - Direct `oxfmt --check --threads=1` for the touched gateway files and SDK hash. The earlier duplicate `PluginDiagnostic` parser failure and the later reset-session type errors are fixed in the PR branch.

commands.command: all feedback from your recent review has been addressed in the latest pushed head `b9ec52b62a`. - preserved reset notification semantics: successful gateway/plugin reset paths now emit through the shared reset flow, and the stale direct handler-only broadcast removal is covered by the replacement `sessions.changed` subscriber test in `src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`. - documented the new public sdk helper: `api.resetsession(key, reason?)` is now documented in `docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md`, including full-mode availability and structured success/failure results. - added the required user-facing changelog entry under `2026.5.2` for the plugin sdk addition. - fixed the ci parser failure from the rebase by removing the duplicate `plugindiagnostic` type import in `src/plugins/registry.ts`. focused local checks run after the final rebase: - `pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts` passed. - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` passed after regenerating the sdk api hash. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/types.ts src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/core.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md changelog.md` passed. the earlier github ci failure was real and related to the duplicate import; that is now fixed and pushed. i also checked the remaining local `test:bundled` diagnostic-listener failure against current `origin/main`, and it reproduces there with the same assertion, so it is not introduced by this pr.

Focused local checks run after the final rebase: - `pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts` passed. - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` passed after regenerating the SDK API hash. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugins/types.ts src/plugins/registry.ts src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/core.ts docs/plugins/sdk-runtime.md CHANGELOG.md` passed. The earlier GitHub CI failure was real and related to the duplicate import; that is now fixed and pushed. I also checked the remaining local `test:bundled` diagnostic-listener failure against current `origin/main`, and it reproduces there with the same assertion, so it is not introduced by this PR.

## Scope

This PR:

- adds optional `resetSession` to `OpenClawPluginApi`

`api.resetSession(key, reason?)`:
